title: Project Time Tracking in D365
subtitle: For Zava Engineering — Project Operations Demo
customer_request: Show me how employees track their time in D365 Project Operations

sections.title: Project Overview
sections.description: Navigate to the Projects area and review an active project

steps.title: Navigate to Projects
steps.tell_before: Let's start by navigating to the Projects area in Dynamics 365. This is the central hub where project managers oversee all active engagements.

actions.description: Click Projects in the left navigation

steps.tell_after: Here we see all active projects. Each row shows the project name, customer, status, and contracting organizational unit — giving managers a quick overview of their portfolio.

value_highlight.title: Portfolio Visibility
value_highlight.description: Project managers get a single view of all engagements across Zava's US, Canadian, and Mexican operations.

steps.title: Open a Project
steps.tell_before: Now let's open a specific project to see its details — the work breakdown structure, team assignments, and financial tracking.

actions.description: Highlight the first project in the list

steps.tell_after: We've opened the project record. Notice the header shows key information at a glance — the project manager, contracting unit, and current status.

sections.transition_text: Now that we've seen the project structure, let's look at how team members actually track their time against project tasks.

sections.title: Weekly Time Entry
sections.description: Create and submit a time entry for a project task

steps.title: Navigate to Time Entries
steps.tell_before: Let's switch to the Time Entries view. This is where your team members log their hours each week — think of it as their digital timesheet.
steps.tell_after: The Time Entry grid shows a calendar-based view. Team members can see their entire week at a glance and quickly enter hours per day.

steps.title: Create New Time Entry
steps.tell_before: Let's create a new time entry. Notice how the system guides the user through selecting the project, task, and role — ensuring accurate cost allocation every time.

actions.description: Click New to create a time entry

actions.tooltip: Select the project for this time

steps.tell_after: The time entry form captures all the information needed for accurate billing — project, task, role, and duration. This data flows directly into cost calculations and client invoices.

value_highlight.title: Accurate Billing
value_highlight.description: Time entries automatically link to role-based price lists, ensuring every hour is billed at the correct rate with zero manual calculation.

steps.title: Submit for Approval
steps.tell_before: Once the time entry is complete, the team member submits it for approval. This triggers the built-in approval workflow — no emails, no spreadsheets, no chasing people down.
steps.tell_after: The time entry is now in the approval queue. The project manager gets notified and can approve or return it — all within D365, creating a complete audit trail.

value_highlight.title: Streamlined Approvals
value_highlight.description: Built-in approval workflows replace email chains and spreadsheets, cutting approval cycle time dramatically.

sections.transition_text: With time entries submitted, let's see what happens on the approval side — how project managers review and approve their team's time.

sections.title: Time Approval Workflow
sections.description: Review and approve submitted time entries

steps.title: Navigate to Approvals
steps.tell_before: Now let's step into the project manager's shoes. They navigate to the Approvals area to review all pending time and expense submissions from their team.
steps.tell_after: The approval view shows all pending items. Managers can approve individually or in bulk, with full visibility into who submitted what, for which project and task.

value_highlight.title: Financial Control
value_highlight.description: Every hour and every expense goes through a governed approval process before hitting financials — protecting revenue and ensuring compliance.

closing_text: We've just walked through the complete time tracking lifecycle in Dynamics 365 Project Operations — from entering time, to approval, to financial impact. This same flow works across all of Zava's entities in the US, Canada, and Mexico, with automatic multi-currency handling and role-based pricing. What questions do you have, or would you like to explore any area in more depth?
